title: XML External Entity (XXE) Processing in Java
summary: DocumentBuilderFactory is instantiated without disabling external entity resolution. When the application parses attacker-controlled XML, the parser may resolve external entities, allowing the attacker to read arbitrary files from the server, perform server-side request forgery (SSRF), or trigger denial of service through recursive entity expansion (Billion Laughs attack).
why_it_matters: XXE vulnerabilities are part of the OWASP Top 10 (A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration) and have been exploited in high-profile breaches. Java's default XML parser configuration enables external entity processing, which means every DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance() call is potentially vulnerable unless explicitly hardened. XXE can exfiltrate sensitive files such as /etc/passwd, application.properties, or cloud credentials from metadata endpoints. In containerized environments, XXE-to-SSRF can access the cloud instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 to steal IAM role credentials.
how_to_fix:
Disable DTDs and external entities on every XML parser instance before parsing any input.

1. Call factory.setFeature("http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl", true) to completely block DTD processing. This is the strongest protection and is recommended for most use cases.
2. If DTDs are required for validation, disable external entities separately: set http://xml.org/sax/features/external-general-entities to false and http://xml.org/sax/features/external-parameter-entities to false.
3. Set factory.setExpandEntityReferences(false) as an additional layer.
4. Apply the same hardening to SAXParserFactory, XMLInputFactory (StAX), TransformerFactory, and SchemaFactory if used.
5. Centralize parser creation in a factory method that enforces secure defaults, so individual call sites cannot forget to set features.
developer_notes: This rule uses a heuristic that flags every DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance() call because statically verifying that setFeature() is called correctly on the same instance before parse() is used requires inter-procedural analysis that the current engine does not yet support. If your code already sets the recommended features, mark the finding as a false positive with a justification comment. Remember that SAXParser, StAX XMLInputFactory, and TransformerFactory all have similar default-unsafe configurations and need equivalent hardening.
verification_checklist: DocumentBuilderFactory instances have disallow-doctype-decl set to true or both external entity features set to false
SAXParserFactory, XMLInputFactory, and TransformerFactory instances are similarly hardened
Parser creation is centralized in a factory method with secure defaults
Integration tests verify that XML payloads containing external entity references are rejected

references.title: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
